name: Islamic Society of Northwest Suburbs of Chicago
mission: Teaching and reading of the holy Quran, daily and Friday congregations, Islamic Education for Children and perform marriages and funeral services
theoryOfChange: By providing a combination of daily worship, full-time and part-time Islamic education, and social services (Zakat/Clinic), the organization aims to develop moral and responsible leaders and maintain economic balance in the Muslim community.
scoreSummary: Islamic Society of Northwest Suburbs of Chicago shows good impact and moderate alignment, with zakat compliance (minor risk deductions applied).
zakatClaimEvidence: The Islamic Society of Northwest Suburbs (ISNS) explicitly accepts zakat donations, stating, "Donate your ZAKAT to ISNS" and providing instructions to "Mail your zakat donation check to the following address: 3950 Industrial Avenue Rolling Meadows, IL 60008, USA." They also mention a "Zakah drop box in the Masjid" and list "Zakat Services" on their website. Additionally, "Zakath" is a selectable category for online donations. (Source: https://isns.org/services/community-services/zakat/)

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Good donor alignment with manageable risk. Evidence quality is solid but still evolving in places.

keyConcerns.headline: Zakat-collecting organization holds 117 months of reserves
keyConcerns.detail: Zakat must be distributed promptly per Islamic jurisprudence. Large reserves raise concerns about timely distribution.

all_citations.claim: The organization reports a 92.5% program expense ratio, $0.01 fundraising efficiency, and 117.3 months of working capital.
The charity is zakat-eligible and maintains specific policies for zakat distribution.
Total revenue reached $3,697,122 for the 2023 fiscal year, supporting programs like Sunday School and funeral services.
The organization provides multiple giving methods for community members to support its religious and educational programs.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization demonstrates strong financial health and high efficiency in its operations, though its overall score is moderated by limited external impact data. Its status as a zakat-eligible institution provides high alignment for Muslim donors seeking traditional community support.
areas_for_improvement: Enhance transparency regarding specific impact metrics and beneficiary outcomes
Increase data availability for third-party evaluators to establish a formal rating

dimension_explanations.alignment: ISNS shows high alignment for donors through its zakat-eligible status and focus on core religious and cultural needs [2]. The organization maintains a strong track record of service delivery, including Sunday School and daily prayers, which are central to the community's religious life [3][4].
dimension_explanations.impact: The society demonstrates high operational capacity with a total revenue of $3,697,122 and a 92.5% program expense ratio [1][3]. While it maintains a massive 117.3 months of working capital, more detailed reporting on specific beneficiary outcomes would further validate its capacity-building efforts [1].

baseline_narrative.headline: The Islamic Society of Northwest Suburbs of Chicago serves as a vital community hub providing comprehensive religious education and essential social services.
strengths: Exceptional program expense ratio of 92.5%, ensuring the vast majority of funds directly support community services
High fundraising efficiency with a cost of only $0.01 per $1 raised
Robust financial stability with 117.3 months of working capital
baseline_narrative.summary: The Islamic Society of Northwest Suburbs of Chicago (ISNS) is a religious non-profit dedicated to the teaching of the Holy Quran, daily congregational prayers, and Islamic education for children [1][3]. The organization facilitates community welfare through zakat distribution and provides essential life-cycle services including marriages and funeral rites [2][3].

case_against.summary: The primary concern involves the <cite id="13">accumulation of 117+ months of reserves</cite>, which is unusual for a zakat-collecting entity that should prioritize prompt distribution.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who want to support long-term Islamic institutional stability and educational infrastructure in the Chicago suburbs through a highly efficient financial model.
donor_motivations: Want to support established Islamic schools like Al Safa Academy
Value organizations with zero-waste fundraising models
Prefer supporting local community hubs with a 50-year history
Seek to fulfill zakat through traditional mosque-based distribution
giving_considerations: The organization holds very high reserves, meaning your gift contributes to long-term solvency
Impact is localized to the Northwest Suburbs of Chicago
Financial reporting is efficient but lacks granular beneficiary data
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking immediate, high-turnover emergency relief or those requiring rigorous third-party impact evaluations.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects a strong theory of change and verified operations, but a lack of public metrics on student outcomes or beneficiary reach.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: ISNS creates impact by providing a centralized location for religious observance and structured Islamic education, which fosters community cohesion and spiritual growth.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that the 92.5% of funds spent on programs are translating into measurable community improvements.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: While revenue is growing rapidly, the organization already holds significant reserves that could fund operations for over a decade.

strengths.detail: The organization directs a <cite id="7">program expense ratio of 92.5%</cite> toward its services, far exceeding the <cite id="11">peer program ratio median of 88.2%</cite>.
ISNS spends only <cite id="8">$0.01 to raise every $1</cite> in contributions, demonstrating a highly efficient, community-driven funding model.
With a history <cite id="1">dating back to 1974</cite>, the society has over 50 years of experience serving the Northwest Suburbs.

strengths_deep_dive: The Islamic Society of Northwest Suburbs of Chicago exhibits extraordinary financial health, characterized by a <cite id="12">3-year revenue CAGR of 39.9%</cite>, which signals strong and growing community trust. This financial trajectory is supported by a massive reserve of <cite id="13">117.3 months of working capital</cite> in working capital, providing a level of long-term sustainability rarely seen in community-based nonprofits. Furthermore, the organization maintains high governance standards with <cite id="14">92.5% independent board members</cite>, ensuring that oversight remains objective and focused on the mission of providing <cite id="15">religious and educational services</cite> to the local Muslim population.
rich_narrative.summary: The Islamic Society of Northwest Suburbs of Chicago (ISNS) serves as a cornerstone for the Muslim community in Illinois, operating as a multi-functional religious and social service institution since its <cite id="1">founding in 1974</cite>. The organization manages a diverse portfolio of services, including <cite id="2">daily and Friday congregational prayers</cite>, the teaching of the Holy Quran, and comprehensive <cite id="3">Islamic education for children</cite>. Beyond religious rites, ISNS functions as a vital social safety net, facilitating <cite id="4">marriages and funeral services</cite> while managing a dedicated <cite id="5">zakat distribution program</cite> for those in need. Financially, the organization demonstrated significant growth in 2023, reporting <cite id="6">total revenue of $4,092,615</cite>, a substantial increase from previous years. A defining characteristic of its fiscal management is an <cite id="7">exceptional program expense ratio of 92.5%</cite>, indicating that all reported functional expenses are directed toward its core community missions. This efficiency is further highlighted by its <cite id="8">fundraising efficiency of $0.01 per $1 raised</cite>, ensuring that donor contributions are not eroded by high administrative or marketing costs. The society maintains a robust physical and educational infrastructure, including the <cite id="9">Al Safa Academy full-time school</cite> and various youth programs like the <cite id="10">Little Scholar Program and After School Maktab</cite>.
